title: ruelingia lanceolata
intro: Ruelingia lanceolata, also known as Lanceleaf Ruellia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States, known for its blue or purple flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, colds, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Ruelingia lanceolata can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them as a tea, and it is also used in some folk remedies to treat skin conditions and wounds.
